table_semantics.justification: Full table structure with <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, <th scope='col'> for plan names, and <th scope='row'> for every feature row. Boolean values use plain text 'Yes'/'No' with no icon-only patterns.

element_choice.justification:
CTAs are elements linking to real URLs (appropriate), prices are in

(not headings), plan cards use

, and the plan list uses
    . No div/span substitutions where semantic elements exist.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1>, followed by <h2> section headings, then <h3> for individual plan names — no skipped levels. Prices are not headings.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA used only where needed: aria-label on <nav>, aria-labelledby on <section> and <article> elements. No redundant role attributes. 'Most Popular' badge is a <span> inside the <h3>, making it naturally accessible without extra ARIA. tabindex='-1' on <main> is correct for the skip-link target.

landmark_structure.justification: Correct use of <header>, <nav aria-label='Primary'>, <main id='main-content'>, <footer>, and labelled <section> elements. Skip link is present and properly implemented.

content_realism.justification: All content is realistic and specific: real pricing tiers, concrete feature values (5 projects, 10GB, 10 team members), and a plausible intro paragraph. No placeholder text.

list_semantics.justification: <ul> used for navigation items and for the plan card collection. Count of plans is meaningful to users, making the list semantics appropriate.

expectations_met: Feature comparison uses <table> for two-dimensional data
Plan names are <th scope='col'> in the header row
Feature names are <th scope='row'> in each data row
Yes/No values are plain text — not conveyed by colour or icons alone
'Most Popular' badge is inside the <h3> heading and read naturally by screen readers
CTAs use <a> elements linking to appropriate URLs
Plan names above the table use <h3> — correct level within the document outline
Prices are not headings; they use
Boolean features use text 'Yes'/'No' without icon-only patterns
additional_observations: The visually-hidden <h2 id='plans-heading'> for the plan cards section is a nice touch — gives the section a programmatic label without cluttering the visual layout.
skip-link implementation is correct with transform-based show-on-focus pattern and tabindex='-1' on the target.
